UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an artist's public profile within the Novart network, showcasing their identity and community engagement metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

artistName.description: The artist's chosen display name or pseudonym.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the artist's profile picture.

biography.description: A short biographical text provided by the artist.

followersCount.description: The total number of other users who follow this artist.

followingCount.description: The total number of other artists this user is following.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

Artwork.title: Artwork
Artwork.description: Represents a piece of digital artwork uploaded by an artist, including its details, public and private URLs, and interaction data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Artwork entity.

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and owns this artwork. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Artwork)

description.description: A detailed description provided by the artist about the artwork.

tags.description: An array of keywords or tags associated with the artwork, used for categorization and discovery.

likedByUsersIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs who have liked this artwork. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Artwork)

publicWatermarkedUrl.description: URL to the publicly accessible, watermarked version of the artwork image, optimized for display.

privateOriginalUrl.description: URL to the private, original high-resolution version of the artwork image, accessible only by the author.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the artwork was first uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the artwork's details were updated.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made by a user on a specific artwork.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

artworkId.description: Reference to the Artwork this comment is associated with. (Relationship: Artwork 1:N Comment)

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who posted this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the comment was posted.

Follow.title: Follow
Follow.description: Represents a follow relationship between two user profiles, where one user is following another.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Follow relationship entity.

followerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the follow. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followingId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is being followed. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N UserProfile)

followedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the follow relationship was established.

definition.description: Stores public profiles for artists. Documents are owned by the user whose ID matches the path parameter. Includes denormalized 'followersCount' and 'followingCount' for community metrics.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (Firebase Auth UID) who owns this profile.

definition.description: Contains details for each piece of digital artwork. Publicly readable, but only the 'authorId' can modify. Includes denormalized 'authorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: artworkId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific artwork.

definition.description: Stores comments made on individual artworks. Publicly readable, any authenticated user can create, but only the 'authorId' can modify their comment. Includes denormalized 'authorId' for authorization independence.

params.name: artworkId
params.description: The unique identifier of the artwork the comment belongs to.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific comment.

definition.description: Records individual follow relationships between users. Publicly readable, but only the 'followerId' can create or delete their own follow relationship. Includes denormalized 'followerId' and 'followingId' for authorization independence.

params.name: followId
params.description: The unique identifier for the follow relationship.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Novart prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   For `Artwork` documents stored in `/artworks/{artworkId}`, the `authorId` field is explicitly denormalized within each artwork document. This allows security rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId` for write, update, and delete operations without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent user document. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule logic.
*   Similarly, for `Comment` documents nested under `/artworks/{artworkId}/comments/{commentId}`, the `authorId` field is included in each comment document. This enables direct authorization checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId`) for comment modifications, again eliminating costly and complex `get()` calls in security rules.
*   For `Follow` documents in the top-level `/follows/{followId}` collection, the `followerId` field is present. This permits security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.followerId` (for creation) and `request.auth.uid == resource.data.followerId` (for deletion) directly, ensuring that only the user initiating the follow can establish or remove it. This avoids any need to inspect parent user documents for authorization.
*   `UserProfile` documents, located at `/users/{userId}`, use path-based ownership. The `userId` in the path segment directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`), providing implicit denormalization for authorization of profile modifications.

**Support for Querying and Access Patterns (QAPs):**
*   **Public Feeds:** The `/artworks` collection is designed for public read access, enabling the main 'Para Ti' feed (Masonry layout) to be efficiently queried and displayed. Queries can filter by `tags` for the 'New Artists' discovery algorithm and order by `createdAt` for recency.
*   **User Profiles:** The `/users/{userId}` collection allows any user to read public profiles, supporting profile viewing and displaying 'followersCount' and 'followingCount'.
*   **Artwork Comments:** The `/artworks/{artworkId}/comments/{commentId}` subcollection provides efficient listing of comments for a specific artwork, crucial for the social interaction feature.
*   **Follow Relationships:** The top-level `/follows/{followId}` collection, although not a subcollection of users, enables straightforward QAPs for fetching a user's followers (`where('followingId', '==', uid)`) or who a user is following (`where('followerId', '==', uid)`). Cloud Functions will listen to changes in this collection to maintain the denormalized `followersCount` and `followingCount` on `UserProfile` documents, optimizing profile queries for these metrics.

This design leverages structural segregation, consistent access modeling, and explicit denormalization to create a robust, secure, and easily debuggable Firestore infrastructure for Novart.
